briefs.title: Burnaby Appoints Chief Election Officer for 2026 Election
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby has chosen Dennis Back as the Chief Election Officer for the 2026 General Local Election. This person will be in charge of running the election.
briefs.source_body:
The General Manager Corporate Services submitted a report recommending the 
appointment of the Chief Election Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General 
Local Election.
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 165 
THAT Dennis Back be appointed Chief Election Officer for the 2026 City of 
Burnaby General Local Election. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: North Road BIA Renewal Approved in Burnaby for 5 Years
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved the renewal of the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) for another five years. A BIA helps local businesses work together to improve their area. The city received enough support to move forward with the renewal.
briefs.source_body:
The General Manager Corporate Services submitted a report providing results for 
the petitions submitted against the proposed North Road Business Improvement 
Area (BIA) renewal bylaw for a 5-year period.
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 166 
THAT the report titled “Certificate of Sufficiency: North Road BIA Renewal” dated 
April 14, 2026 be received for information; 
THAT the Certificate of Sufficiency dated April 14, 2026, certified by the 
Corporate Officer and included as Attachment 1 to this report, be received; and 
THAT a copy of this report be sent to the President of the Burnaby North Road 
Business Improvement Association. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority: Approve Corporate Maintenance for 2026
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby, as the sole shareholder of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA), approved several corporate maintenance tasks for 2026. These include setting the annual reference date, appointing an auditor (an independent firm that checks the BHA's finances), receiving financial statements, and appointing BHA directors.
briefs.source_body:
The General Manager Planning and Development and the Deputy Chief 
Administrative Officer/Chief Financial Officer submitted a report seeking Council 
approval with respect to certain annual shareholder’s corporate maintenance 
matters for 2026 on behalf of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA).
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 167 
THAT the City, as outlined in the report titled “BHA – Shareholder’s Corporate 
Maintenance Matters for 2026”, dated April 14, 2026, as sole shareholder of the 
Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA): and; 
• authorize the business of the annual general meeting of the BHA for the 
2026 year to be transacted through an unanimous shareholders 
resolution; 
• select December 12, 2026 as the annual reference date for the BHA; 
• appoint KPMG as auditor of the BHA until the next annual general meeting 
of the BHA, and the directors of the BHA be authorized to determine the 
remuneration payable to the auditor, and to give the auditor notice of 
appointment as required by the Business Corporations Act (British 
Columbia); 
• receive the BHA’s financial statements for the period ending December 
31, 2025; and 
• appoint the individuals identified in section 3.3 as directors of the BHA in 
accordance with the terms set out in section.
THAT the Shareholder Representative be directed to execute such shareholders 
resolutions as necessary to give effect to the foregoing resolutions. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority's 2025 Financials and 2026 Budget Approved
briefs.summary: The Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA) had its 2025 financial statements and 2026 operating budget approved. The financial statements cover June 14, 2024, to December 31, 2025. The council received the reports for information.
briefs.source_body:
The Deputy Chief Administrative Officer/Chief Financial Officer submitted a report 
presenting the Burnaby Housing Authority’s (BHA) 2025 Annual Financial 
Statements and 2026 Annual Operating Budget.
*Councillor Dhaliwal left at 5:29 pm returned at 5:32 pm 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 168 
THAT the report titled “BHA – 2025 Financial Statements and 2026 Operating 
Budget”, dated for April 14, 2026, be received for information; 
THAT the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation’s (BHA) 2025 Annual Financial 
Statements, which includes the audited Consolidated Financial Statements, for 
the period from June 14, 2024 to December 31, 2025 be received; and 
THAT the BHA’s 2026 Annual Operating Budget be received. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby to Amend Bylaws for E-Bike and Electric Kick Scooter Programs
briefs.summary: Burnaby will change its bylaws to allow for an E-Bike Share Pilot Program and address the province's Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program. The changes will affect the Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw, the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw, and the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about the new rules for e-bikes and e-scooters in Burnaby.
briefs.source_body:
The General Manager Engineering submitted a report proposing amendments to 
the Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw and the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw to 
implement the E-Bike Share Pilot Program and address the provincial Electric 
Kick Scooter Pilot Program within the City.
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 169 
THAT the City Solicitor be authorized to bring forward amendments to the 
following bylaws to implement the E-Bike Share Pilot Program and address the 
provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program: 
• Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961; 
• Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979; and 
• Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, 
 
as described in the report titled “Proposed Bylaw Amendments to Address E-Bike 
Share Pilot Program and Provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program”, dated 
April 14, 2026. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby to Submit Housing Target Progress Report to BC Ministry
briefs.summary: Burnaby will send its Housing Target Progress Report to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs. This is required by the Housing Supply Act and Housing Supply Regulation. The report shows the city's progress toward meeting provincial housing targets.
briefs.source_body:
The General Manager Planning and Development submitted a report presenting 
the Housing Target Order and obtain Council authorization to forward Burnaby’s 
Housing Target Progress Report Form to the BC Ministry of Housing and 
Municipal Affairs, as required by the Housing Supply Act and Housing Supply 
Regulation.
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 170 
THAT the report titled “Provincial Housing Target Order 6-Month Interim Progress 
Report”, dated April 14, 2026, be received for information; and 
THAT the General Manager Planning and Development be authorized to submit 
the Housing Target Progress Report Form, as found in Attachment 2, to the BC 
Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby to Participate in Building Benchmark BC Year 7
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby will participate in Building Benchmark BC for the seventh year. This program helps the city measure and improve the energy efficiency and environmental performance of its buildings. No additional funding is needed beyond the existing 2026 budget.
briefs.source_body:
The General Manager Planning and Development submitted a report providing 
Council with an update on Building Benchmark BC and seeking authorization to 
participate in Year 7.  
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 171 
THAT staff be authorized to participate in Building Benchmark BC Year 7, as 
described in the report titled “Building Benchmark BC”, dated April 14, 2026, with 
no additional funding required beyond the existing 2026 budget. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Grants FortisBC Right-of-Way for Burnaby Mountain Infrastructure
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved a Statutory Right-of-Way (legal permission to use land) for FortisBC. This allows FortisBC to install, operate, and maintain natural gas infrastructure on parts of 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way. The infrastructure will service the Burnaby Mountain Conservation Area.
briefs.source_body:
The General Manager Lands and Facilities submitted a report seeking Council 
approval to grant Statutory Right-of-Way in favour of FortisBC Energy Inc. 
(FortisBC) over a portion of 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way to 
permit the installation, operations and maintenance of natural gas works.
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 172 
THAT the granting and execution of Statutory Right-of-Way over a portion of 
7950 Pandora St. and 1200 Gaglardi Way in favour of FortisBC Energy Inc. for 
the installation, operations, and maintenance of infrastructure required to service 
Burnaby Mountain Conservation Area, as outlined in the report titled “7950 
Pandora St & 1200 Gaglardi Way – Burnaby Mountain – Statutory Right-of-Way 
for FortisBC”, dated April 14, 2026, be approved. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Approves Contract for New Modular Housing Development
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved a contract with Echelon Creative Inc. to build a modular four-plex housing unit. The project, located at 8315 – 14th Avenue, is a pilot project with an estimated cost of $1,354,463, including taxes. Final payment will depend on the actual amount of goods delivered and their unit prices.
briefs.source_body:
The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council 
approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the development of a modular four-plex 
housing unit as a pilot project.
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 173 
THAT a contract award to Echelon Creative Inc. for an estimated cost of 
$1,354,463 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled “CA – Development of 
Modular Housing Four-Plex Unit at 8315 – 14th Avenue”, dated April 14, 2026, be 
approved; and 
THAT final payment will be based on actual quantities of goods delivered and 
unit prices quoted. 
CARRIED 
(Opposed: Councillor Dhaliwal)

briefs.title: Culvert Replacement Contract Awarded in Jerry Rogers Creek
briefs.summary: The city has awarded a contract to HANNA Infrastructure Ltd. for the Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement Project. The contract is for an estimated $2,404,076, including taxes. The final payment will depend on the actual amount of work done and the agreed-upon prices.
briefs.source_body:
The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council 
approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert 
Replacement Project.
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 174 
THAT a contract award to HANNA Infrastructure Ltd., for an estimated cost of 
$2,404,076 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled “CA – (CB-8039) Jerry 
Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement” dated April 14, 2026 be approved; and 
THAT final payment will be based on the actual quantities of services delivered 
and unit prices as tendered. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement Contract Approved
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved a contract for Drake Excavating (2016) Ltd. to replace a culvert (a tunnel carrying a stream under a road) and pave the Central Valley Greenway. The contract is for an estimated $1,360,391, including taxes. The final payment will be based on the actual work done.
briefs.source_body:
The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council 
approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Central Valley Greenway Culvert 
Replacement and Paving Project 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 175 
THAT a contract award to Drake Excavating (2016) Ltd., for an estimated cost of 
$1,360,391 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled “CA – (CB-3264) 
Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement and Paving Works”, dated April 
14, 2026 be approved; and 
THAT final payment will be based on the actual quantities of services delivered 
and unit prices as tendered. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Cellular Voice and Data Services Contract Change Approved
briefs.summary: The city approved a contract change with Rogers Communications for cellular voice and data services. The estimated cost is $450,000, including taxes. The final payment will be based on the actual services used and hourly rates.
briefs.source_body:
The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council 
approval for a Contract Change (CC) for Telephony - Cellular Voice and Data 
Services.
Staff undertook to provide additional information on the contract costs 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 176 
THAT a contract change to Rogers Communications Canada Inc. for an 
estimated cost of $450,000 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled “CC - 
Telephony - Cellular Voice and Data Services”, dated April 14, 2026 be approved; 
and 
THAT final payment will be based on actual quantity of services delivered and 
hourly rates as submitted. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: HVAC System Maintenance Contract Approved for City-Owned Buildings
briefs.summary: The city approved a contract change with Just Mechanical Ltd. for HVAC (heating, ventilation, and air conditioning) maintenance and repair at city buildings. The contract is for an estimated $579,600, with final payment based on services provided and hourly rates.
briefs.source_body:
The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council 
approval for a contract change (CC) for HVAC system maintenance & repair 
services at various City-owned buildings.
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 177 
THAT a contract change to Just Mechanical Ltd. for an estimated total cost of 
$579,600 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled “CC – HVAC System 
Maintenance & Repair Services”, dated April 14, 2026, be approved; and 
THAT final payment will be based on the actual quantity of services delivered 
and hourly rate submitted. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Approves Cisco WebEx Contact Centre Licence Renewal
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved a three-year contract renewal with Long View Systems Corporation for its Cisco WebEx Contact Center license subscription. The contract is for an estimated $394,942, including taxes. The final payment will depend on the actual amount of services delivered.
briefs.source_body:
The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council 
approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for Cisco WebEx Contact Center License 
Subscription.
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 178 
THAT a three-year contract renewal to Long View Systems Corporation for an 
estimated amount of $394,942 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled “CR 
- Cisco WebEx Contact Center License Subscription”, dated April 14, 2026, be 
approved; and 
THAT final payment will be based on the actual quantity of goods and services 
delivered and unit prices proposed. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Fire Protection Contract Renewal Approved by Council
briefs.summary: The city council approved renewing a contract with A.W Fireguard Supplies (1991) Ltd. for fire protection systems and equipment installation, maintenance, and repair services. The contract is estimated to cost $1,666,061, including taxes. The final payment will depend on the actual services provided and the hourly rate.
briefs.source_body:
The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council 
approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for Fire Protection Systems and 
Equipment Installation Maintenance and Repair Services.
*Councillor Wang left at 6:38 pm and returned at 6:41 pm 
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 179 
THAT a contract renewal to A.W Fireguard Supplies (1991) Ltd. for an estimated 
total cost of $1,666,061 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled “CR – Fire 
Protection Systems and Equipment Installation Maintenance and Repair 
Services”, dated April 14, 2026, be approved; and 
THAT final payment will be based on the actual quantity of services delivered 
and hourly rate submitted. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Approves Language for Burnaby Lake Overpass Plaque
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved the wording for a plaque that will be installed at the Burnaby Lake Overpass. The plaque will commemorate the opening of the overpass.
briefs.source_body:
The General Manager Corporate Services submitted a report seeking Council 
approval for the wording and content for the commemorative plaque to be 
installed as part of the opening of Burnaby Lake Overpass.
MOVED AND SECONDED 
 
Resolution No. 2026 - 180 
THAT the language for the plaque to be installed at Burnaby Lake Overpass 
outlined in section 3.1 in the report titled “Burnaby Lake Overpass Official 
Opening Plaque,” dated April 14, 2026, be approved. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby to Review Financial Practices to Avoid Tax Hikes, Service Cost Increases
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby will have an external reviewer assess its financial planning, operations, and investment choices. The goal is to protect the city's finances and prevent large tax increases or rising costs for local businesses and residents.
briefs.source_body:
The Financial Management Committee (FMC) submitted a report seeking 
Council approval to engage an external reviewer to help safeguard the City's 
assets and financial standing, and to mitigate the risk of significant tax increases 
and rising service costs for businesses and residents.
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 181 
THAT engaging a qualified external reviewer to assess the City’s financial 
planning, operating practices, and capital investment priority setting, as 
discussed at the Financial Management Committee meeting held on March 17, 
2026, be approved; and 
THAT the reviewer liaise with the Financial Management Committee and deliver 
findings and recommendations directly to Council. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby Council to Consider Rezoning Bylaw for 20-Storey Apartment
briefs.summary: The Planning and Development Committee has asked the Council to consider a rezoning bylaw. If approved, it would allow a 20-storey apartment building with 215 units to be built at 5035 Beresford Street. The Council will also consider a Housing Agreement, if needed.
briefs.source_body:
The Planning and Development Committee (PDC) submitted a report seeking 
Council authorization to forward REZ #25-11 to First and Second Reading at a 
future Council meeting. The purpose of the proposed zoning bylaw amendment 
is to permit the construction of a 20-storey multiple-family apartment 
development comprised of 215 residential units above underground parking.  
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 182 
THAT a Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-11 be prepared and advanced to First and 
Second Reading at a future Council meeting; 
THAT a Housing Agreement, if required, be authorized according to the terms 
outlined in Section 3.4 of the report titled “REZ #25-11 - 5035 Beresford Street – 
Proposed 20-Storey Apartment Development (Royal Oak Community Plan)”, 
dated March 31, 2026, of the Planning and Development Committee meeting, 
and the City Solicitor be authorized to bring forward a Housing Agreement Bylaw; 
and 
THAT the items listed in Attachment 3 to the report be established as 
prerequisites to consideration of Final Adoption of the Rezoning Bylaw for REZ 
#25-11. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

briefs.title: Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) Bylaw Cancelled After Petitions
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby cancelled Bylaw No. 14797, which would have created a Business Improvement Area (BIA) for North Road. The decision was made after the City received enough petitions against the bylaw.
briefs.source_body:
MOVED AND SECONDED 
Resolution No. 2026 - 185 
THAT due to sufficient petitions received by the City against Burnaby Business 
Improvement Area (Burnaby North Road) Bylaw 2026, Bylaw No. 14797 be now 
abandoned. 
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY
